Chrysojasminum humile, also known as Jasmine, is a flowering plant in the olive family (Oleaceae) that is widely cultivated for its fragrant flowers and essential oils. The health benefits of Jasmine include its ability to promote relaxation, reduce stress and anxiety, and improve sleep quality due to its sedative and calming properties. The bioactive constituents of Jasmine include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Jasmine include teas, infusions, and essential oils, which are used in aromatherapy and traditional medicine to promote relaxation and well-being.
